mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (measured by GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes) signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters produce negative forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate borrowing capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: When trigger condition is met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term exceeds 0.05 but directional accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, which depresses corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the earnings impact propagates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE level and 60-day forward SPX returns is less negative than -0.02, or elevated unemployment periods produce positive forward returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth acceleration signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market participants rapidly reprrice growth expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth months produce negative forward returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth combined with housing starts (HOUST) expansion signals robust consumer and residential demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days are positive due to the joint signal of broad-based demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% AND HOUST MoM growth > 2%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 48% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is below 0.04 despite individual series showing positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) deceleration below 100K new jobs per month signals weakening labor demand and potential earnings headwinds for consumer-facing sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and recession risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100K for 2+ consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns is less negative than -0.01, or weak payroll months produce positive forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 4.0% combined with stable or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals monetary policy easing that reduces equity discount rates and improves borrowing conditions for corporations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth declines by > 0.3pp from prior month
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.2 million units annualized signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic weakness, which reduces construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.2M units annualized for 2+ consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST level and 60-day forward SPX returns is less negative than -0.02, or periods of weak housing starts produce positive forward returns in > 56% of instances.
